field_evidence.critical_condition_basis: The solvent composition was 47:53 ACN/H2O (v:v) as determined to be the critical conditions for PEG.
field_evidence.critical_component: The solvent composition was 47:53 ACN/H2O (v:v) as determined to be the critical conditions for PEG.
field_evidence.column_name: The column used was an octadecyl silica column with a pore size of 120 Å and particle size of 5 μm with a 250×4.6 mm inner diameter.
field_evidence.mobile_phase_solvents: The solvent composition was 47:53 ACN/H2O (v:v) as determined to be the critical conditions for PEG.
field_evidence.mobile_phase_ratio: The solvent composition was 47:53 ACN/H2O (v:v) as determined to be the critical conditions for PEG.
field_evidence.pore_size: The column used was an octadecyl silica column with a pore size of 120 Å and particle size of 5 μm with a 250×4.6 mm inner diameter.
field_evidence.flow_rate: The crude samples were dissolved as 1 mg mL⁻¹, and 10 μL of the sample was injected at a flow rate of 0.5 mL min⁻¹.

conditions.critical_condition_basis: The solvent composition was 47:53 ACN/H2O (v:v) as determined to be the critical conditions for PEG.
conditions.physical_address: Department of Chemistry, Wayne State University, Detroit, MI 48202, USA; BAM Federal Institute for Materials Research and Testing, D-12489, Berlin, Germany; BAM Federal Institute for Materials Research and Testing, D-12489, Berlin, Germany
conditions.notes: LCCC performed on Agilent 1100/1200 Series HPLC. ELSD nebulizer 90°C, evaporator 110°C (detector temps, not column).
conditions.corresponding_author_name: Sarah Trimpin; Steffen M. Weidner; Jana Falkenhagen
